source: ICH S6(R1), 2012; Nishimoto et al., PMC3306224; Tocilizumab SmPC (anti-TNF cross-reference)
description: Expected findings for anti-TNF-alpha monoclonal antibodies (adalimumab, infliximab, golimumab, certolizumab). TNF-alpha is a central mediator of innate immunity; blockade produces immunomodulatory effects including acute phase protein suppression and lipid changes. Cynomolgus monkey is the primary species. TNFR-Fc (etanercept) is pharmacologically equivalent — SME should select this profile for those studies. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: Significant reduction; mirrors anti-IL-6 effects (TNF-alpha is a potent IL-6 inducer)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: N/A — expected PD endpoint. Adversity relates to downstream infection risk.
expected_findings.rationale: TNF-alpha blockade reduces serum IL-6 rapidly, secondarily reducing CRP and SAA. (Nishimoto et al., PMC3306224)
TNF-alpha suppression of IL-6 leads to lipid profile normalization; paradoxically raises total cholesterol. (Nishimoto et al., 2010)
Same mechanism as cholesterol — reversal of TNF-driven lipoprotein catabolism.

expected_findings.description: Lymphocyte subset shift (Treg increase, Th17 decrease)
expected_findings.rationale: TNF-alpha plays a role in T-cell survival; blockade shifts Treg/Th17 balance.
